title: Troubleshoot and Recommend - Knowledge Check
description: Confirm you understand the observe-diagnose-remediate loop.

questions.title: What did the Chaos Spell actually do?
questions.content: castChaosSpell caused the Todo app outage. Technically, what happened?

options.text: The todoapp deployment was scaled to 0 replicas
options.explanation: No replicas means no running pods, so the service is unavailable.
The node stayed Ready; only the app was scaled down.
Dynatrace kept monitoring - that is how you saw the outage.

questions.explanation: Scaling to 0 replicas removes running pods; dispelChaos scales back to 1.

questions.title: How would you catch this automatically next time?
questions.content: In a real environment, what is the recommended Dynatrace-native way to be notified and (where safe) auto-remediate?

options.text: A Dynatrace workflow triggered by the Davis problem
options.explanation: Workflows react to problems and can notify or auto-remediate.

options.text: Manually refreshing the app every minute

options.explanation: That hides incidents rather than handling them.

questions.explanation: Davis detects the availability problem; a workflow turns that into notification or remediation.
hints: Think automation reacting to a Davis problem.
